title: Anyone using Monday.com to track EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating System)
excerpt: I would love to see how you have built it out for Annual Planning, Quarterly Rocks, Tasks, Scorecard, Level 10 Agenda, etc.

posts.author: Atticus (Atticus Rominger)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love to see how you have built it out for Annual Planning, Quarterly Rocks, Tasks, Scorecard, Level 10 Agenda, etc.

posts.author: Atticus (Atticus Rominger)
posts.cookedHtml: Did not! We are still using Excel to track EOS. Works fine but they don’t talk to eachother the way they could in Monday.com? Want to collaborate on building some boards?

posts.author: colleen928 (Colleen Bonniol)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! I would love to collaborate on this. I think others would find it helpful as well. my email is colleen@modestudios.com - Feel free to reach out directly, be sure to introduce yourself in the first email.

We’d love to help you with this! Feel free to share with us a little more context on what you’re looking to set up and we’d be happy to make any suggestions.

If not we’d love to see the outcome of your collaborative board building, as it sounds like this could be hugely helpful for other users too!

Best,

Dani

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey all. I think I also asked about this a while back. Due to the way recurring tasks work elegantly in Clickup!, this was fairly easy to achieve there. On Monday, I ran out of time to do this but would love to ‘freeload’ if someone else get’s it all working. The challenge, if I recall, was getting the L10 notes and to-dos to carry forward week to week since Monday doesn’t really have a native recurring tasks.

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mchinsky

If by “ran our of time” you mean that your trial expired, you can always contact support@monday.com and request a trial extension to help give you a bit more time to play around with everything!

As for recurring tasks, we do have ways for you to set them up as needed! Check out this article for more details:

  • How To Create Recurring Tasks

You can also always use an Automation that will push the due date of an item by a week when it’s reached so that it keeps all of the original updates section notes, etc. but with a new due date:

image1366×588 45.2 KB

Otherwise, we’d love to hear what you’ve come up with @Atticus!

Cheers,
Jenna

posts.author: monday_user (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml: I am experimenting with using one of the Documents in Monday for the L10 meeting and then embedding IDS, ToDo and Rocks into it which would be tracked in boards. Issue I am currently struggling with is the goal of having each of these other boards be company wide but only letting users see the items that are applicable to them (have a department name in each item and it shows up via filter on that departments L10 meeting). I am hoping item level permissions could work for this although it looks like the current iteration means I would have to have every user in the department added to each item… maybe this could be done with an automation based on department selection. Anyway, interested in what others come up with.

posts.author: StacyJ (Stacy Jones)
posts.cookedHtml: stacy@hollywoodbranded.com

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml: On a related note how are you all doing with EOS? For your top level owners, about how many L10’s do you participate in per week?

posts.author: Atticus (Atticus Rominger)
posts.cookedHtml: @mchinsky We have been using EOS for about 7 years. We are a small organization, but have flexed in size a couple of times and have used L10’s differently over time. Initially our top leadership team had one L10. After a quarter, we rolled it our to our Directors, and we all had 1 L10 a week (less than 10 people). We then added team L10’s for about 3 different teams. I (integrator) attended those three plus the Directors Meeting. We then shuttered a couple of programs, and now our entire team participates in 1 L10 a week (about 15 people). It is excellent for information sharing and accountability. Since COVID however, and with such a large group from so many different disciplines, the IDS portion is pretty anemic. We rarely take on weighty topics anymore, so problem solving happens in smaller groups and we don’t have the diversity of thought we once did. That’s my $.02. Hope it helps.

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml:
About how many employees do you have?

The problem I’m seeing is we are 5 partners in our company and there seems to be too many l10’s where more than 1 partner is attending such that the average partner is in 8-12 hours of EOS meetings per week (probably averages more like 16 if you add in the time for quarterly and annuals).

It just seems to eat away at the available time to either sell, market or deliver services by those partners so whatever efficiencies are created by the EOS process are eaten up by the costs of the extra staff doing the things the partners used to do.

posts.author: Atticus (Atticus Rominger)
posts.cookedHtml: Sounds familiar. When I was participating in 4 meetings a week it felt like way too much. It would have been better if our Directors were reliably bringing headlines, issues, etc. from their Level 10’s to our Director Level 10, but for various reasons there always seemed to be an information gap that I could only fill by being in each meeting. I guess there’s a decision to be made there for top level leaders in EOS: More information/More Time in Meetings vs. Less Detail/Less Time In Meetings.

@Atticus

posts.author: richardmorris (Richard Morris)

Hi - I am an EOS Implementer but also the visionary for a non profit I cofounded in the UK. In my non-profit we use Monday.com to track our EOS stuff so happy to answer any questions if helpful.
Officially, EOS has an agreement with 90.io and is also preparing to launch EOS One (i.e. not beta) at some point soon ish. But it’s important that people use whatever platform makes the most sense for them.
Richard

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: One of the main reasons I haven’t seriously looked into implementing an EOS based product (as a developer) on monday.com is the licensing of the trademarks. EOS is such a litigious company that I determined there is no viable way to implement anything based on the process and succeed since you can’t so much as mention EOS anywhere or even allude to using it a product for EOS as a commercial entity.
